summary.intent: Refactor the Gossamer Claude Code session viewer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing and viewing Claude sessions.
summary.outcome: Successfully built a working VS Code extension with a sessions list panel, session detail tabs (opening to the right), native checkpoint tree view with diff support, FTS search via QuickPick, new/resume session terminal spawning, VS Code theme integration, and onboarding flow for repos without Entire.io.

repo: Gossamer's architecture: Entire.io CLI writes session data to a git branch â†’ gossamer indexes that into SQLite via Prisma â†’ server exposes HTTP API â†’ UI reads from API
Entire.io presence is detected via `.entire/settings.json` at repo root
Repo registry is at `~/.gossamer/config.json` with `localPath`, `dbPath`, `name`, `remote` fields
`CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` maps checkpointId to gitOid; `ShadowSession.cwd` gives the working directory of a session
The `/api/branch-log` endpoint uses exact `findRepo` matching which breaks when `cwd` is a subdirectory of the repo root â€” fixed with prefix-matching `findRepoForPath` in `src/config.ts`
Commit messages are retrieved via `git log --format=%s -1 <gitOid>` and require trying multiple candidate repo directories when session cwd is unavailable
The monorepo uses npm workspaces: `packages/core` (`@gossamer/core`), `packages/vscode`, and `app` (Electron, intentionally broken)

code.finding: Was only listing specific files in `include` (extension.ts, GossamerPanel.ts), causing SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to never be compiled â€” root cause of blank diff views
Must use `execFileSync('which', ['node'])` for system Node.js path, NOT `process.execPath` which points to VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js â€” critical for native module version compatibility
`server_ready` must be re-sent via `onDidChangeViewState` when a tab becomes visible, because moving a tab to a new window causes the webview to reload from scratch

Auto-scroll should check scroll position inline at effect time (`el.scrollHeight - el.scrollTop - el.clientHeight < 80`) rather than via a separate scroll listener â€” Mantine's ScrollArea populates the viewport ref asynchronously after mount
Vite `root` and `outDir` must be absolute paths (using `resolve(__dirname, ...)`) when the package is run via npm workspaces, otherwise paths resolve relative to repo root CWD
repoName was derived from global `basename(repoDir)` giving every session the same name; must use `basename(session.cwd)` per-session
In-memory TextDocumentContentProvider with `gossamer-diff:` URI scheme avoids temp files and produces inherently read-only documents; file extension must be in the URI path for syntax highlighting

workflow: VS Code extension host runs on Electron's bundled Node.js â€” native modules compiled for system Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 141) will fail with a version mismatch (MODULE_VERSION 140); spawn the server as a child process using system node to avoid this entirely
Vite outputs ESM; webview `<script>` tags must have `type='module'` or the browser silently ignores them

VS Code automatically injects CSS custom properties into webviews reflecting the active theme; use `--vscode-editor-background`, `--vscode-panel-border`, etc. and set Mantine's `forceColorScheme` from `document.body.classList`
`vscode.window.createQuickPick` is the idiomatic VS Code pattern for search overlays, supports `QuickPickItemKind.Separator` for grouped results
For read-only virtual documents in diffs, prefer `TextDocumentContentProvider` over temp files on disk
`vscode.ViewColumn.Beside` reliably opens tabs to the right of the currently focused panel

friction: tsconfig.extension.json `include` list caused silent compile omission of new files â€” no error, just missing runtime modules, very hard to diagnose
Vite relative `root` path resolving against workspace CWD rather than package directory was non-obvious and produced a confusing 'Cannot resolve entry module index.html' error
VS Code's `process.execPath` pointing to Electron rather than system Node was a hidden footgun; required `which node` as workaround
Mantine's ScrollArea viewport ref is populated asynchronously, causing scroll listeners attached in a `[]`-dep effect to silently do nothing
Moving a webview panel to a new window triggers a full reload â€” any one-time setup messages (like `server_ready`) must be re-sent on `onDidChangeViewState`

The in-memory content provider approach was attempted first but abandoned for temp files due to URI encoding issues, then reinstated once the correct approach was understood â€” caused multiple debugging rounds
Multiple context window overflows during the session requiring summaries, disrupting flow
open_items: Show session name in sessions table instead of session ID (deferred when user renamed the session mid-conversation)
Electron `app/` is intentionally broken after the monorepo refactor â€” should be formally deprecated or removed
`packages/core` only exposes module API; a CLI tool (`gossamer-index`) was mentioned as a possible future export

The `better-sqlite3` native module rebuild story for `packages/core` is unresolved â€” it works because the server is spawned externally, but `npm install` in CI/production contexts may need `npm rebuild`
Onboarding flow checks for `.entire/settings.json` but does not validate that the Entire.io CLI hook is actually configured â€” a repo could have the config file but not be writing to git
The new session terminal sends `/rename <name>` after a 2-second delay â€” fragile if claude takes longer to start; no confirmation the rename succeeded

Branch-log deduplication in CheckpointTreeProvider is by checkpointId string set â€” if the same checkpoint appears in both session and branch groups with different data shapes, only one copy is shown
